summary.summary: The SAST analysis of the RemoteFilter repository (branch: upload) for WITTE Automotive's FAZIT project found no security vulnerabilities across all conducted tests. The codebase is an Arduino-based embedded IR remote filter application that receives, filters, and retransmits infrared signals for home automation purposes. Given the nature of the firmware — running on a microcontroller with no network connectivity, authentication surface, or API layer — the applicable attack surface is minimal. No insecure design patterns or other vulnerability classes were identified.

test_results.summary: No insecure design vulnerabilities were identified. The embedded firmware nature of the application renders common web/API-based design weaknesses (authentication, session management, rate limiting, etc.) entirely inapplicable.
